data.title: China announces increase of additional tariffs on US goods to 125%
data.description: Both the dollar and risk trades are getting hit on the headlines here as China continues to go blow for blow with Trump on the tariffs front. But at this point, does the number really matter? More to come..

data.title: Chinese coal buyers try to escape contracts as spot prices hit four-year low
data.description:
Chinese coal buyers are trying to renegotiate long-term contracts, eyeing better deals on the spot market where prices are at four-year lows, three sources familiar with the matter said, adding pressure to China's cash-strapped coal sector.

Weak demand has pushed spot market prices below the level of existing long-term contracts, said Li Wei, chairman of Shandong Energy Group, one of China's largest state-owned miners, in written comments to Reuters.

That is "putting pressure on contract fulfilment" in a sector where about half of miners are operating in the red, he said.

China's domestic price for medium-grade 5,500 kcal/kg coal was 676 yuan ($92.31) per metric ton on April 10, the lowest since March 2021, according to the Bohai-Rim Bay thermal coal price index.

"Many end-users are unwilling to execute their long-term contracts," said a China-based trader who declined to be identified due to company policy.

An employee responsible for coal procurement at an industrial firm said that the company's contracts would likely be renegotiated but "if we really can't come to an agreement, we might have to postpone the shipment."

China's coal sector has been buffeted by a warmer-than-usual winter that curbed heating demand, as well as over-supply in the domestic market - a combination that led two industry bodies in February to call for limits on output and imports.

Buyers are digging in their heels on contracts signed with domestic miners, the sources said, while import term deals - which make up a small portion of China's coal supply - appear unaffected thus far.

China imported a record 542.7 million tons of coal in 2024.

Power plants in China usually sign long-term contracts covering some 20-50% of their overall coal needs, buying the rest through the spot market, the trader said.

On April 9, the China Coal Transportation and Distribution Association, an industry body, said relevant government departments were stepping in to ensure the fulfilment of long-term contracts.

The China-based trader said that would help improve compliance with contracts to some extent.

"However, it will not have any effect on prices in the short term. Demand is too poor."

($1 = 7.3233 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: China 'Not Afraid of Any Unjust Suppression,' Says Xi
data.description:
By Tracy Qu

China is "not afraid of any unjust suppression," Chinese President Xi Jinping said in his first public comment since the recent escalation in trade tensions with the U.S.

"There are no winners in a tariff war. Confronting the world will only lead to isolation," Xi said at a meeting with Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez in Beijing, state media Xinhua reported on Friday.

"For over 70 years, China has relied on self-reliance and hard work, not on favors from others, and is not afraid of any unjust suppression," Xinhua quoted Xi as saying. "No matter how the external environment changes, China will stay confident, remain focused and handle its own affairs well."

Tensions between U.S. and China have intensified over the past weeks as the two sides trade tit-for-tat tariff threats.

Write to Tracy Qu at tracy.qu@wsj.com

data.title: U.K. Growth Could Continue Despite Tariff ShockMarket Talk
data.description: U.K. economic growth is likely to remain resilient and withstand the shock of U.S. tariffs, Berenberg's Andrew Wishart says in a note. The comments come after data showed the U.K. economy grew by an unexpectedly strong 0.5% during February. "Given the U.K. is well insulated from the direct impact of tariffs because it sells few goods to the U.S. market, we suspect growth will weather the tariff shock relatively well." The economy, nonetheless, faces the risk of weakening consumer and business confidence due to worsening trade disputes, he says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: APB Resources To Not Proceed With Name Change
data.description: APB Resources decided to not change the company's name from "APB RESOURCES BERHAD" to "DMC CAPITAL BERHAD," after due consideration, according to a Thursday Malaysian bourse filing.

data.title: Indian tyre stocks gain after CLSA starts coverage with bullish ratings
data.description:
** Analysts at CLSA initiate coverage on Indian tyre maker, Apollo Tyres , with "high conviction outperform" rating; MRF and CEAT with "outperform"

** Sets TP on APLO, MRF, CEAT at 566 rupees, 128,599 rupees, and 3,493 rupees, respectively

** Shares of APLO, MRF, CEAT up 4.1%, 3%, 3.2%, respectively, amid broader market rally after U.S. President Donald Trump announced 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs on Wednesday

** Indian tyre market, valued at $12 billion, moving up profitability curve with capacity addition focused on higher-margin passenger car radial tyres vs lower-margin truck and bus tyres - CLSA

** Says sector is at the bottom of margin cycle; gross margins to improve on softening commodity prices

** Sees the sector relatively insulated from impact of U.S. tariff implementation due to limited dependency on exports

data.title: Aeon: Company Unit Welcia to Be Merged with Tsuruha through Share Swap on Dec. 1
Patriot Battery Metals' Cesium Drill Results, Metallurgical Test Work May Add 'Significant Value', Says Euroz Harteys
data.description:
Patriot Battery Metals' awaited further drill results and with metallurgical test work could add "significant" value in time, said Euroz Hartleys in a Thursday note.

The company confirmed on Thursday that drilling at its Shaakichiuwaanaan project in Canada resulted in the discovery of wide high-grade cesium pegmatite, with four samples greater than 20% cesium oxide.

Drill results yielded three meters at 9.4% cesium oxide, including one meter at 22.4% cesium oxide, and 5.9 meters at 11.2% cesium oxide, including one meter at 22.7% cesium oxide.

The research firm said it currently does not assume any value for cesium in its model for the company and awaits further results.

Euroz Hartleys kept Patriot Battery Minerals' speculative buy rating and maintained its AU$1.24 price target.

The company's shares rose 2% in Friday's close.

data.title: European Gas Rises But Remains on Track for Significant Weekly LossMarket Talk
data.description: European natural-gas prices rise in early trade, but are poised for a weekly loss of more than 7% as the market grapples with the potential impact of weaker economic growth and trade disruptions. The benchmark Dutch TTF contract is up 1.4% to 33.75 euros a megawatt hour after U.S. President Trump paused reciprocal tariffs on most countries for 90 days and raised duties on China. Meanwhile, Europe faces an extremely challenging stockpiling season this year. European gas transmission group Entsog warned that it is "critical" to start replenishing inventories as soon as possible and said the continent will require a higher volume of LNG compared with previous summers. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: Billecta sets new record for billing transactions in March
data.description:
SaveLend Group AB’s subsidiary, Billecta AB (“Billecta”), continues to grow. In March 2025, the company’s billing platform processed over 550,000 billing transactions – a new record and an increase of nearly 10% compared to the same month last year.

This development highlights the platform’s capacity and scalability, as well as the value Billecta delivers to its customers by offering a fully automated billing solution – from invoice creation to payment reconciliation.

Since Billecta was acquired in 2018, SaveLend Group has supported the company’s growth journey. Today, the billing platform serves as a trusted partner to clients across the nonprofit sector, member organizations, and the private business community. Among Billecta’s users are organizations such as Cancerfonden, the Swedish Medical Association, Akavia, Storage365, and Qasa.

“Surpassing 550,000 transactions in a single month is a clear sign of the reliability and efficiency of our solution. It’s the result of a strong team, a sharp offering, and close collaboration with our customers,” says Mats Röjdmark, CEO of Billecta AB.

With continued growth and high customer satisfaction, Billecta is well-positioned to capture additional market share and continue its expansion throughout 2025 and beyond.

data.title: REG - Bank of Ireland Grp - Directorate Change
data.description:
RNS Number : 6895E Bank of Ireland Group PLC 11 April 2025  

The Governor and Company of the Bank of Ireland and Bank of Ireland Group PLC

(together "Bank of Ireland")

Change in Directorate

11 April 2025

__________________________________________________________________________________

Bank of Ireland announces that Ms Eileen Fitzpatrick will retire as an Independent Non-Executive Director at the 22 May 2025 AGM, having served on the Board since May 2019.

During her tenure on the Board, Eileen has served as a member of the Board Audit, Remuneration, and Nomination & Governance Committees, and more recently held the role of Sustainability Committee Chair.

Eileen was the inaugural holder of the Board's Workforce Engagement Director role from January 2020 to February 2024 establishing a firm foundation that continues to be built upon in ensuring, in conjunction with the Group CEO, that the views and interests of colleagues are considered in Board discussions. 

Eileen was also appointed as a Group nominee on the Board of Davy and as Chair of its Risk and Compliance Committee following the acquisition of Davy by Bank of Ireland in 2022 and she has agreed to remain on the Davy Board for a brief period to facilitate conclusion of related succession activities.

Ms Margaret Sweeney, who joined the Board in October 2023, will succeed Eileen as the Sustainability Committee Chair.

Bank of Ireland Chairman, Mr. Akshaya Bhargava, acknowledged Eileen's contribution to the Board since her appointment in 2019, saying that "Eileen has made a significant contribution to the Group across the wide range of roles held during her tenure and her considered perspective has greatly benefited the Board and colleagues at both Bank of Ireland and Davy".

This announcement is made in accordance with Rule 6.1.5 of the Euronext Dublin Listing Rules and Rule 6.4.6 of the London Stock Exchange's Listing Rules.

Ends/

data.description:
Oslo, 11 April 2025 - The annual general meeting of NEXT Biometrics Group ASAwill be held on 9 May 2025, at 09:00 CEST at Stortorvet 7, 0155 OSLO, Norway.

All shareholders are encouraged to vote electronically prior to the meeting orissue a proxy to the chairman (or the person he/she appoints) and not attendphysically.

Go to www.nextbiometrics.com/investors/general-meetings/ to cast your vote.Thedeadline for prior voting is 8 May 2025 at 09:00 CEST.

All documents regarding the annual general meeting are available atwww.nextbiometrics.com/investors/general-meetings/. The Annual Report for 2024is available atwww.nextbiometrics.com/investors/financial_reports_presentations/.

About NEXT Biometrics NEXT provides advanced fingerprint sensor technology that deliversuncompromisedsecurity and accuracy for the best possible user experience in the payment andfintech, government ID, access control and office and notebook markets. Thecompany's patented NEXT Active Thermal ® principle allows the development oflarge, high-quality fingerprint sensors with superior security level. NEXTBiometrics Group ASA is headquartered in Oslo, with sales, support, anddevelopment operations in Seattle, Taipei, Tokyo, New Delhi and Shanghai.

data.title: MARCH VOLUMES: EASTER TIMING DIFFERENCE INCREASED FREIGHT
data.description:
INVESTOR NEWS no. 15 - 11 April 2025

March 2025 volume numbers are compared to 2024 impacted by the Easter holidayperiod falling in April this year compared to March in 2024. In general, thisincreases freight volumes and decreases passenger volumes compared to March2024.

Ferry - freight: Total volumes in March 2025 of 3.8m lane metres were 4.1%above2024 and up 3.6% adjusted for route changes. For Q1 2025, the growth rateswere -0.2% and -0.5%, respectively.

North Sea volumes were above 2024 as most routes carried higher volumes.Mediterranean volumes were below 2024 due to increased ferry competition inthecorridor between Türkiye and Italy.

Channel and Baltic Sea volumes were both above 2024 driven mainly by theEastertiming difference. Strait of Gibraltar volumes were also above 2024 driven bygeneral market growth and the Easter impact.

For the last twelve months 2025-24, the total transported freight lane metresincreased 5.9% to 41.5m from 39.2m in 2024-23. The increase was 2.0% adjustedfor route changes.

Ferry - passenger: The number of passengers in March 2025 was 46.3% below 2024and down 29.5% adjusted for route changes. For Q1 2025, the growth rateswere -27.4% and -12.1%, respectively.

The adjusted monthly decrease was driven by mainly the Easter timingdifference.The number of cars was 38.9% below 2024 and down 30.8% adjusted for routechanges.

For the last twelve months 2025-24, the total number of passengers increased26.9% to 6.3m compared to 5.0m for 2024-23. The increase was 2.3% adjusted forroute changes.

DFDS ferry volumes

March   Last-12-months-------------------------------------------- ---------------------------- Freight 2023 2024 2025 Change   2023 2024 2025 Change

Lane metres, '000 3,544 3,603 3,752 4.1%   40,751 39,184 41,514 5.9%-------------------------------------------- ----------------------------

Passenger 2023 2024 2025 Change   2023 2024 2025 Change

Passengers, '000 236 482 259 -46.3%   4,110 4,999 6,343 26.9%-------------------------------------------- ----------------------------

DFDS reports monthly ferry volumes for freight and passengers to provideinsightinto the development of volume trends in our European route network enablingtrade and travel in and around Europe. The April 2025 volume report isexpectedto be published on 12 May 2025 at around 10.00am CET.

Contact

Torben Carlsen, CEO +45 33 42 32 01

Karen Boesen, CFO +45 20 58 58 40

Søren Brøndholt Nielsen, IR +45 33 42 33 59

Dennis Kjærsgaard, Media +45 42 30 38 47

About DFDS

We operate a transport network in and around Europe with an annual revenue ofDKK 30bn and 14,000 full-time employees.

We move goods in trailers by ferry, road & rail, and we offer complementaryandrelated transport and logistics solutions.

We also move car and foot passengers on short sea and overnight ferry routes.

DFDS was founded in 1866 and headquartered and listed in Copenhagen.

data.title: North Macedonia's pension funds assets down 1% m/m in Mar
data.description:
SKOPJE (N. Macedonia), April 11 (SeeNews) - The net assets managed by North Macedonia's three mandatory pension funds came in at 165.5 billion denars ($3 billion/2.7 billion euro) in March, down 1.1% compared with the end of the previous month, official data showed.

KB Prv Otvoren Zadolzitelen Penziski Fond (KBPz) ranked first with 80.5 billion denars in net assets at the end of March, followed by Sava Penziski Fond (SAVAz) with 71.7 billion denars and Triglav Otvoren Zadolzitelen Penziski Fond (TRIGLAVz) with 13.3 billion denars, according to figures released by North Macedonia's pension insurance supervision agency, MAPAS, on Thursday.

KBPz had 282,573 clients at the end of March, SAVAz account holders numbered 262,854 and TRIGLAVz clients totalled 69,586.

KBPz is managed by KB Prvo Penzisko Drustvo, co-owned by Slovenia's Skupina Prva and North Macedonia's Komercijalna Banka, according to MAPAS data.

SAVAz, managed by Sava Penzisko Drustvo, is owned by Slovenian reinsurer Sava Re, whereas TRIGLAVz is managed by Triglav Penzisko Drustvo and owned by Slovenian insurer Triglav.

North Macedonia has a population of 1.8 million.

(1 euro = 62.06 denars)

https://news.cision.com/uie-plc/r/notice-of-annual-general-meeting-of-uie-plc-%2Cc4130736

UIE Notice of AGM 2025 appendices

data.title: Barclays Keeps Enel at Overweight, Forecasts 'Decent' Q1 Performance
data.description:
Barclays reiterated its overweight rating and 8.50-euro price target on Enel (ENEL.MI), expecting the Italian energy company to post a "decent" first-quarter performance in its upcoming results on May 8.

Analysts forecast year-over-year net earnings growth of between 2% and 3% for the three-month period, excluding the impact of Enel's de-consolidation of its Peruvian operations. However, ordinary net profit is expected to decline 2% to 1.90 billion euros.

"We forecast profit before taxes and minorities of 3,223mn euros (down 1% yoy), given our expectation of flat net financing costs, due to forecast stable net debt levels. We expect an average tax rate of 30% and we forecast net debt of 55bn euros at the end of Q1," the research firm wrote.

Among segments, network activities are projected to deliver a 1% increase in EBITDA to 2.08 billion euros, primarily driven by growth in the Italian and Latin American markets. Conversely, the contribution from Peru is expected to decline by 59 million euros.

data.title: India's Sun Pharma up after US Appeals Court paves way for hair loss drug launch
data.description:
** Sun Pharmaceuticals Industries rises 2.43% to 1,692.4 rupees

** U.S. Court of Appeals vacates preliminary injunction imposed by a U.S. District Court in 2024 for co's hair loss drug, allowing co to launch the drug in the market

** "We believe the ruling clears a major roadblock for Sun," says Jefferies

** Nomura says drug launch now likely in FY26 vs previous estimate of Q4 FY27

** Adds, launch could be at risk as Sun is still litigating the patent infringement claim that led to injunction

** On the day, pharma stocks index climb 2.72% and benchmark Nifty index rises 2.2% after U.S. pauses reciprocal tariffs for 90 days

** Including session's gains, SUN shares up 5.4% in the last 12 months

Market Chatter: ASEAN Ministers to Not Retaliate Against US Tariffs
data.description:
ASEAN economic ministers agreed not to retaliate against recent US tariffs and instead called for open dialogue to resolve trade tensions, Taipei Times reported Friday.

According to the joint statement, the report noted that retaliation would harm the region, as the US remains ASEAN's largest investor and second-biggest trading partner. The ministers expressed concern over the tariffs but emphasized the importance of maintaining a stable economic relationship.

Vietnam and Cambodia were hit with the highest duties, while others like Malaysia and the Philippines faced lower rates. The meeting was chaired by Malaysia, which holds the rotating ASEAN chair. Meanwhile, the EU also paused its planned tariffs, following a 90-day suspension by the US, the publication said.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: West River Eyes MYR42 Million IPO
data.description:
West River (KLSE:WESTRVR) is targeting to raise 41.9 million ringgit via its initial public offering on the Ace Market of Bursa Malaysia.

The IPO of the mechanical and electrical (M&E) engineering services provider involves a public issue of 71.5 million shares and an offer for sale of 35.8 million shares at 0.39 ringgit apiece, according to a Thursday Malaysian bourse filing.

Proceeds will be used for expansion and establishment of fiber optic communication networks infrastructure, repayment of bank borrowings, and capital expenditure, it said.

data.title: French Stocks Extend Gains, Still Set for Weekly Loss
data.description:
The CAC 40 rose 0.8% to 7,184 on Friday, marking its second consecutive session of gains, as investor sentiment improved following the suspension of EU and US tariffs.

The European Union announced a temporary 90-day pause on its planned retaliatory tariffs against the US, mirroring Washington's own reprieve and signaling a willingness on both sides to return to the negotiating table.

However, caution remained as the US pressed ahead with tariff hikes on Chinese imports, a move that rekindled fears of a prolonged trade war and continued to cast a shadow over global growth prospects.

Most stocks traded higher, with STMicroelectronics leading the index with a 2.6% gain, followed by Kering, whose shares rose 2.2%, and Pernod Ricard, with shares up 1.7%.

On the downside, Stellantis NV was the worst performer among the few decliners, with shares falling 2.1%.

Despite Friday’s gains, the index remains on track to post a weekly loss.
